title: Vendor diligence intake note
content:
# Vendor diligence intake note

Matter: AtlasPay / Northlake hosted payments integration
Packet received: 2026-06-01
Review target: closing-ready summary for the 2026-06-04 vendor approval meeting

The packet combines the vendor data-room export, legal working files, security
review attachments, and diligence-team intake material. The documents labeled
`current` are the materials the intake team expects to be relevant for the
approval meeting. Older negotiation drafts and working redlines are retained for
traceability.

Requested output from the review:

* identify the governing commercial and data-protection terms;
* summarize support, availability, incident, security, insurance, and renewal
  terms;
* identify items still open for approval;
* cite the packet sources used for each major conclusion.

title: Master Services Agreement
content:
# Master Services Agreement

Document ID: AP-NLP-MSA-2026-05-14
Parties: AtlasPay, Inc. and Northlake Payments LLC
Execution date: 2026-05-14
Effective date: 2026-06-01
Status: Executed

## 1. Services

Northlake Payments LLC will provide hosted payment-orchestration services,
including API routing, payment tokenization, transaction status callbacks,
settlement-file generation, and account-management support for the AtlasPay
merchant platform. Services are limited to the modules purchased on an order
form or statement of work.

## 2. Term and renewal

The initial term begins on 2026-06-01 and continues for twenty-four months. The
agreement renews for successive one-year renewal terms unless either party gives
written notice of non-renewal at least sixty days before the end of the then
current term.

## 3. Fees and payment

Fees are set out in the executed order form. Undisputed invoices are due thirty
days after receipt. A good-faith invoice dispute must be raised within fifteen
days after receipt and the undisputed portion remains payable.

## 4. Assignment and corporate transactions

Neither party may assign the agreement without the other party's prior written
consent, except that either party may assign the agreement to an affiliate or to
a successor in connection with a merger, reorganization, or sale of substantially
all assets, provided the successor is not a direct competitor of the non-assigning
party, assumes the assigning party's obligations in writing, and receives notice
within ten business days after closing. A transfer to a direct competitor of the
non-assigning party requires prior written consent.

## 5. Confidentiality

Confidential information may be used only to perform or receive the services.
The receiving party must protect confidential information using at least the
same degree of care it uses for its own similar information, and not less than a
reasonable degree of care.

## 6. Termination

Either party may terminate for material breach if the breach is not cured within
thirty days after written notice. AtlasPay may terminate an affected order form
if Northlake misses the production API availability target for three consecutive
months. Termination for convenience is available only when an order form says so.

## 7. Liability

The general liability cap is twelve months of fees paid or payable under the
affected order form. The cap does not limit payment obligations, confidentiality
breaches, data-protection indemnities, fraud, willful misconduct, or a party's
IP infringement indemnity obligations.

## 8. Survival

Confidentiality, payment obligations, accrued credits, audit records, ownership,
limitations of liability, indemnities, dispute-resolution terms, and data
return or deletion obligations survive termination.

title: Data Processing Addendum
content:
# Data Processing Addendum

Document ID: AP-NLP-DPA-2026-05-14
Parties: AtlasPay, Inc. and Northlake Payments LLC
Execution date: 2026-05-14
Status: Executed

## 1. Roles

AtlasPay is the controller or business for merchant account and transaction
data. Northlake is the processor or service provider. Northlake processes
personal data only on documented instructions from AtlasPay, including the
instructions in the agreement, order forms, support tickets, and approved
implementation runbooks.

## 2. Data categories

The services may process merchant contact data, payer identifiers, transaction
metadata, tokenized payment references, settlement identifiers, support-case
metadata, and integration logs. Full card primary account numbers are not stored
by Northlake for the AtlasPay implementation.

## 3. Security incident notice

Northlake must notify AtlasPay without undue delay and in any event within
forty-eight hours after confirming a security incident involving AtlasPay data.
The notice must include known facts, affected systems, data categories, mitigation
steps, and a contact for incident coordination. Follow-up notices must be sent
as material information becomes available.

## 4. Subprocessors

Northlake must maintain a current subprocessor list and provide at least thirty
days' advance notice before authorizing a new subprocessor for AtlasPay data.
AtlasPay may object on reasonable data-protection grounds during the notice
period.

## 5. Return and deletion

At termination or expiration, Northlake must return available AtlasPay data in a
commercially reasonable export format and delete remaining copies within
forty-five days, except backup copies retained under standard backup cycles or
records required by law.

## 6. International transfers

Transfers outside the United States and European Economic Area must use the
Standard Contractual Clauses or another valid transfer mechanism, together with
documented transfer-impact review where required.

title: Security Addendum
content:
# Security Addendum

Document ID: AP-NLP-SEC-2026-05-14
Parties: AtlasPay, Inc. and Northlake Payments LLC
Execution date: 2026-05-14
Status: Executed

## Program requirements

Northlake will maintain an information-security program aligned to SOC 2 Trust
Services Criteria and PCI DSS controls applicable to tokenized payment routing.
The program includes executive oversight, written policies, employee training,
annual risk assessment, vendor review, and change-management controls.

## Technical controls

AtlasPay production data must be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or an
equivalent industry-standard algorithm. Data in transit must use TLS 1.2 or
higher. Administrative access requires multi-factor authentication. Production
access is limited to approved personnel with a business need and is reviewed at
least quarterly.

## Vulnerability management

Northlake must run authenticated infrastructure scans at least monthly and an
external penetration test at least annually. Critical vulnerabilities affecting
AtlasPay production systems must be remediated within fifteen days unless
AtlasPay approves a documented exception. High vulnerabilities must be remediated
within thirty days.

## Logging and monitoring

Security logs for production systems must be retained for at least twelve months.
Northlake maintains alerting for privileged access, authentication anomalies,
malware detections, and payment-routing service degradation.

## Audit evidence

Northlake will provide SOC 2 Type II evidence, bridge letters, penetration-test
attestations, and policy summaries under NDA upon reasonable request.

title: Service Level Schedule 2
content:
# Service Level Schedule 2

Document ID: AP-NLP-SLA2-2026-05-14
Parties: AtlasPay, Inc. and Northlake Payments LLC
Execution date: 2026-05-14
Status: Executed

## Covered services

The production payment-orchestration API, tokenization API, webhook delivery
service, and settlement-file API are covered services for AtlasPay. Sandbox,
beta, reporting backfills, and third-party payment processor outages are not
included in the availability calculation.

## Availability target

Northlake will provide 99.9 percent monthly uptime for covered production APIs.
Availability is calculated monthly using Northlake's monitoring system and
excludes planned maintenance, emergency maintenance, force majeure, AtlasPay
misconfiguration, and third-party processor outages outside Northlake's control.

## Maintenance

Planned maintenance requires at least seven days' advance notice and should be
scheduled outside North American business hours where commercially practicable.
Emergency maintenance notice will be provided as soon as practicable.

## Service credits

If monthly uptime is at least 99.0 percent but below 99.9 percent, AtlasPay may
claim a service credit equal to five percent of affected monthly recurring fees.
If monthly uptime is below 99.0 percent, AtlasPay may claim a service credit
equal to ten percent of affected monthly recurring fees. Credits are capped at
ten percent of affected monthly recurring fees for the month.

## Support response

Priority 1 production incidents receive a target response within thirty minutes.
Priority 2 production incidents receive a target response within two business
hours. Status updates for Priority 1 incidents are provided at least every hour
until service restoration or mitigation.

title: Order Form: Payments API and Tokenization
content:
# Order Form: Payments API and Tokenization

Order form ID: AP-NLP-OF-2026-05-20
Customer: AtlasPay, Inc.
Vendor: Northlake Payments LLC
Execution date: 2026-05-20
Status: Executed

## Purchased modules

AtlasPay purchases Northlake's hosted payment-orchestration API, tokenization
API, webhook delivery service, settlement-file API, premium production support,
and implementation assistance for the AtlasPay merchant platform.

## Implementation timeline

The target production go-live date is 2026-07-15, subject to completion of
implementation testing, security evidence review, and AtlasPay's internal change
approval.

## Volumes and fees

The committed volume tier is 2.4 million transactions per month. Overage pricing
begins after the committed tier. The order form does not include card-present
payment terminal processing, chargeback representment services, or consumer
wallet identity verification.

## Data location

Northlake will process AtlasPay production data in the United States and the
European Union using the approved subprocessor list. Any additional production
processing location requires the subprocessor notice process in the DPA.

## Special terms

Premium support is included for production launch through 2026-09-30. Renewal
pricing for later terms follows the MSA renewal process unless a later order
form is signed.

title: SOC 2 Type II Bridge Letter
content:
# SOC 2 Type II Bridge Letter

Vendor: Northlake Payments LLC
Report period bridged: 2026-04-01 through 2026-05-31
Related SOC 2 Type II report period: 2025-10-01 through 2026-03-31
Prepared for: AtlasPay, Inc.
Date: 2026-05-31

Northlake Payments LLC confirms that management is not aware of material
changes to the control environment for the payment-orchestration platform during
the bridge period from 2026-04-01 through 2026-05-31. The control environment
remained aligned to the controls described in the SOC 2 Type II report covering
2025-10-01 through 2026-03-31.

This bridge letter is intended to accompany the SOC 2 Type II report provided
under NDA. It does not replace the full SOC 2 Type II report and does not
include the independent service auditor's detailed testing results.

Management contact: compliance@northlake.example

title: Certificate of Insurance Summary
content:
# Certificate of Insurance Summary

Insured: Northlake Payments LLC
Certificate date: 2026-05-22
Broker: Hartwell Risk Partners
Certificate holder: AtlasPay, Inc.

## Cyber liability

Carrier: Rook Mutual Specialty
Policy period: 2026-02-01 through 2027-02-01
Aggregate limit: USD 5,000,000
Retention: USD 100,000

## Technology errors and omissions

Carrier: Rook Mutual Specialty
Policy period: 2026-02-01 through 2027-02-01
Aggregate limit: USD 3,000,000
Retention: USD 75,000

## Notice

The broker summary states that the certificate holder receives thirty days'
notice of cancellation where required by policy terms. The certificate is a
summary of coverage and does not amend the policies.

title: External Penetration Test Attestation
content:
# External Penetration Test Attestation

Vendor: Northlake Payments LLC
Assessment window: 2026-04-08 through 2026-04-19
Assessor: Redfield Security Labs
Scope: Northlake production payment-orchestration API, tokenization API,
administrative portal, and webhook delivery service.

Redfield Security Labs completed an external penetration test for the scoped
Northlake systems. No critical findings remained open at report issuance. Two
high findings were remediated and retested before 2026-04-30. Three medium
findings remain in normal remediation tracking and are not assessed as launch
blocking for AtlasPay's hosted API implementation.

The detailed technical report is available under NDA through Northlake's
compliance portal.

title: Diligence Open Items Register
content:
# AtlasPay / Northlake Diligence Open Items Register

Register date: 2026-05-29
Prepared by: AtlasPay Vendor Risk and Legal Operations

## Open items

1. Full SOC 2 Type II report
   Status: Requested.
   Detail: The bridge letter is in the packet, but the full auditor report must
   be reviewed under NDA before final vendor-risk signoff. Owner: Vendor Risk.
   Target: 2026-06-06.

2. Subprocessor list update for LedgerWorks EU backup processing
   Status: Pending final confirmation.
   Detail: The DPA permits new subprocessors with thirty days' notice. Northlake
   confirmed LedgerWorks is used for EU backup processing but the customer-facing
   subprocessor list still needs the final entry and notice date. Owner:
   Privacy Counsel. Target: 2026-06-07.

3. Incident runbook addendum
   Status: In review.
   Detail: AtlasPay requested a one-page escalation-contact addendum mapping
   Priority 1 contacts, webhook degradation contacts, and after-hours bridge
   procedures. Owner: Security Operations. Target: 2026-06-05.

4. PCI DSS Attestation of Compliance renewal
   Status: Draft expected.
   Detail: The 2025 AOC remains valid through 2026-06-30. Northlake expects to
   provide the 2026 AOC before the AtlasPay production go-live date. Owner:
   Compliance. Target: 2026-06-20.

## Closed items

Data residency FAQ: Closed 2026-05-24. Northlake confirmed United States and
European Union processing locations for AtlasPay production data.

Source-code escrow request: Waived 2026-05-27 after product and legal review
because AtlasPay is buying hosted API services rather than deployed software.

title: Master Services Agreement draft
content:
# Master Services Agreement Draft

Date: 2026-04-03
Status: Draft

This working draft used a twelve-month initial term, required prior
consent for all assignments including mergers, and set a liability
cap at six months of fees. The assignment language and cap were
revised in the executed agreement.

title: Data Processing Addendum draft
content:
# Data Processing Addendum Draft

Date: 2026-04-08
Status: Draft

This draft used a seventy-two-hour incident notice period and a
ninety-day deletion period. The final DPA changed incident notice
and deletion timing.

title: Service Level Schedule working redline
content:
# Service Level Schedule Redline

Status: Working redline

Earlier language proposed 99.5 percent monthly uptime and a five
percent service-credit cap. The executed schedule changed the
target and credit structure before signature.

content:

support terms.
